---
title: Narrative Voice
tags: [voice, style]
---

# Narrative Voice

Voice is the set of choices that stay constant no matter which passage
a reader lands on: point of view, tense, the narrator's vocabulary, the
things the narrator notices first in a room. A branching story that
lets voice drift between passages reads as several authors stitched
together, even when a single model wrote every line.

## Anchoring on the window

The surest way to hold voice steady across hundreds of passages is to
keep the neighboring, already-written prose in view while drafting the
next passage — the immediate window is a better anchor than any
abstract style description, because it shows the actual sentence
rhythm rather than describing it.

## Second person present tense

Many interactive fiction pieces default to second person present tense
because it collapses the distance between reader and protagonist; the
choice, once made, must be held everywhere, including in passages
written weeks apart.
